title: DUX v9.5 Behavior Object
description: Atomic, testable user action serving as instrumentation anchor - defines what adoption means and maps to loggable signals

object_type.description: Object type discriminator - always 'Behavior' for Behavior objects

user_enablement.description: User enablement statement: '[Persona] is able to [task/action]' - This core enablement statement defines what behavior we need to make happen for users.

signals.description: Loggable system events that prove this behavior occurred (e.g., 'report_config_event_created'). PROMPT GUIDANCE: Always provide at least one signal - behaviors must be measurable. Use specific, technical event names that developers can implement.

id.description: The unique ID of the referenced Flow object.

reference_context.description: Why this Flow object is related — shared user, phase, behavior, or problem context.

flow_ids.description: Array of Flow objects that contain this behavior - behaviors exist within flow context

acceptance_criteria.description: Clear, testable criteria that define successful completion of this behavior. PROMPT GUIDANCE: Include at least one criterion that directly relates to the defined signals (e.g., 'User successfully triggers report_config_event_created signal'). Make criteria specific and measurable.

evidence_maturity.description: Progressive evidence maturity tier: 01_assumptive (inferred, no direct support), 02_anecdotal (1-2 qualitative signals), 03_early_signal (recurring signal, no quant), 04_balanced_signal (qualitative + quantitative), 05_triangulated (cross-method validation)

evidence.description: Array of Provenance object IDs that support this behavior's importance and definition

tags.description: System-derived tags generated by NotebookLM DUX scan - not manually set
